test.name: Valid evaluations[] entry passes cleanly (false-positive guard)
test.description: Run schema validation against mid-research-flynn-with-evaluation, the clean scenario plus one well-formed evaluations[] entry (ev_001: focus 'on-demand', target_type 'question' -> q_001, verdict 'looks_solid', valid ISO timestamp, superseded_by null). The validator should return no errors. This is the only test that exercises the validateEvaluations code path — every prior positive test left evaluations[] empty — so it guards against false positives on the ev_ prefix, the evaluation enums, and target_id/superseded_by resolution.

input.user_message: Validate the project files for schema compliance.

judge_context: Should report that both project files are valid with no errors

Should NOT flag the evaluations[] entry: ev_001 uses the correct 'ev_' prefix, valid closed-enum values (focus 'on-demand', target_type 'question', verdict 'looks_solid'), a target_id (q_001) that resolves to an existing question, a valid ISO 8601 timestamp, and superseded_by null — all legitimate
Reporting 'both project files are valid' is the correct and complete response for this read-only skill
